datasets.name: mme_signaling_events
datasets.cardinality_requirements: Ensure 2-5 mme_host values share the same (cluster_id, datacenter) combination for split-brain detection. Ensure handoff failures occur across multiple adjacent cell pairs for cascade analysis.

queries.name: Proactive Subscriber-Impacting Incident Detection with 5-15 Minute Early Warning
queries.pain_point: Reactive incident response overwhelming help desk operations - lack of predictive alerting causes customer-reported issues to flood help desk before engineering awareness
queries.description: Detect subscriber-impacting incidents 5-15 minutes before help desk call spikes by identifying clusters with 500+ unique IMSIs experiencing service failures within 1-hour windows, using adaptive percentile-based thresholds
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | WHERE attach_result == "FAILURE" OR tau_result == "FAILURE" OR gtp_tunnel_result == "FAILURE" | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS unique_imsi_count = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), total_failures = COUNT(*), revenue_impacting_failures = COUNT(*) WHERE revenue_impacting == true BY time_bucket, cluster_id, datacenter | INLINESTATS p75_imsi = PERCENTILE(unique_imsi_count, 75), p90_imsi = PERCENTILE(unique_imsi_count, 90) BY cluster_id | EVAL severity = CASE(unique_imsi_count >= 1000, "CRITICAL", unique_imsi_count >= 500, "HIGH", unique_imsi_count >= p90_imsi, "MEDIUM", "LOW") | WHERE unique_imsi_count >= 500 | KEEP cluster_id, datacenter, severity, unique_imsi_count, total_failures, time_bucket, revenue_impacting_failures | SORT unique_imsi_count DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: MME Resource Exhaustion and Memory Leak Detection Before Failover
queries.pain_point: MME software bugs and resource exhaustion impacting subscriber capacity - memory leaks and CPU exhaustion degrade performance before failover
queries.description: Identify MME hosts showing signs of resource exhaustion through statistical analysis of CPU/memory utilization combined with NAS message processing latency degradation, detecting memory leaks before they trigger failover events
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | STATS avg_nas_latency = AVG(nas_processing_latency_ms), p95_nas_latency = PERCENTILE(nas_processing_latency_ms, 95) BY mme_host, cluster_id | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | EVAL heap_utilization_pct = (mme_hosts.heap_used_mb / mme_hosts.heap_max_mb) * 100, thread_pool_utilization_pct = (mme_hosts.thread_pool_active / mme_hosts.thread_pool_max) * 100 | INLINESTATS avg_cpu = AVG(mme_hosts.cpu_utilization_pct), stddev_cpu = STD_DEV(mme_hosts.cpu_utilization_pct), avg_memory = AVG(mme_hosts.memory_utilization_pct), stddev_memory = STD_DEV(mme_hosts.memory_utilization_pct) BY cluster_id | EVAL cpu_z_score = (mme_hosts.cpu_utilization_pct - avg_cpu) / COALESCE(stddev_cpu, 1), memory_z_score = (mme_hosts.memory_utilization_pct - avg_memory) / COALESCE(stddev_memory, 1) | WHERE cpu_z_score > 2.5 OR memory_z_score > 2.5 OR heap_utilization_pct > 85 OR thread_pool_utilization_pct > 90 | EVAL risk_level = CASE(cpu_z_score > 3 AND memory_z_score > 3, "CRITICAL_FAILOVER_IMMINENT", cpu_z_score > 2.5 OR memory_z_score > 2.5, "HIGH_DEGRADATION", "MODERATE") | KEEP mme_host, cluster_id, risk_level, cpu_z_score, memory_z_score, avg_nas_latency, p95_nas_latency, heap_utilization_pct, thread_pool_utilization_pct | SORT cpu_z_score DESC | LIMIT 15

queries.name: Cell Tower Handoff Cascade Failure Analysis with Adjacent Cell Impact
queries.pain_point: Dropped calls and interrupted data sessions during mobility events - handoff failures between eNodeBs and gNodeBs especially in high-mobility scenarios
queries.description: Identify handoff cascade failures by analyzing failure patterns across adjacent cell pairs, detecting when failures propagate through neighbor cells and categorizing root causes to pinpoint problematic cell tower configurations
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | WHERE handoff_result == "FAILURE" | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS handoff_failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_imsi_affected = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), failure_causes = VALUES(handoff_failure_cause) BY time_bucket, source_cell_id, target_cell_id, cluster_id | LOOKUP JOIN cell_sites ON source_cell_id = cell_sites.cell_id | EVAL source_cell_name = cell_sites.cell_name, source_enodeb = cell_sites.enodeb_id, source_technology = cell_sites.technology | DROP cell_sites.cell_id, cell_sites.cell_name, cell_sites.enodeb_id, cell_sites.technology | LOOKUP JOIN cell_sites ON target_cell_id = cell_sites.cell_id | EVAL target_cell_name = cell_sites.cell_name, target_enodeb = cell_sites.enodeb_id, target_technology = cell_sites.technology | WHERE handoff_failure_count >= 20 | EVAL handoff_pair = CONCAT(source_cell_name, " -> ", target_cell_name), technology_pair = CONCAT(source_technology, " -> ", target_technology) | KEEP source_cell_id, target_cell_id, handoff_pair, technology_pair, handoff_failure_count, unique_imsi_affected, time_bucket, failure_causes | SORT handoff_failure_count DESC | LIMIT 25

queries.name: SS7 Signaling Attack and Roaming Fraud Detection via Behavioral Analysis
queries.pain_point: SS7 signaling attacks and rogue network access attempts - vulnerable to location tracking, SMS interception, and fraudulent roaming attacks
queries.description: Detect SS7/Diameter signaling attacks through semantic pattern matching of suspicious message sequences combined with subscriber behavioral analysis, identifying location tracking attempts, SMS interception, and fraudulent roaming before financial impact
queries.esql_query: FROM ss7_diameter_events | WHERE SEMANTIC(event_pattern_description, "location tracking attempts SMS interception unauthorized roaming fraudulent authentication") OR is_suspicious == true OR anomaly_score > 0.7 | STATS attack_event_count = COUNT(*), unique_attack_types = COUNT_DISTINCT(attack_category), avg_anomaly_score = AVG(anomaly_score), suspicious_networks = VALUES(source_network) BY imsi, cluster_id | LOOKUP JOIN subscriber_profiles ON imsi | EVAL combined_risk_score = (avg_anomaly_score * 0.6) + (subscriber_profiles.fraud_risk_score * 0.4) | INLINESTATS p90_risk = PERCENTILE(combined_risk_score, 90) | WHERE attack_event_count >= 3 OR combined_risk_score >= p90_risk | EVAL threat_level = CASE(combined_risk_score > 0.85, "CRITICAL_BLOCK_IMMEDIATELY", combined_risk_score > 0.7, "HIGH_INVESTIGATE", "MEDIUM_MONITOR") | KEEP imsi, threat_level, combined_risk_score, attack_event_count, unique_attack_types, subscriber_profiles.subscriber_tier, subscriber_profiles.roaming_status, suspicious_networks | SORT combined_risk_score DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Core Network Split-Brain Detection and Signaling Storm Prevention
queries.pain_point: Core network split-brain scenarios and signaling storms - network partitions create duplicate authoritative instances causing signaling floods
queries.description: Detect core network split-brain conditions by identifying multiple MME hosts in the same cluster simultaneously processing conflicting state for the same subscribers, combined with SCTP association churn patterns indicating network partitions before region-wide signaling storms occur
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_events | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp) | STATS unique_mme_hosts = COUNT_DISTINCT(mme_host), event_count = COUNT(*), unique_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), sctp_state_changes = COUNT(*) WHERE sctp_state IN ("ESTABLISHED", "CLOSED", "SHUTDOWN_PENDING") BY time_bucket, cluster_id, datacenter | INLINESTATS avg_events = AVG(event_count), stddev_events = STD_DEV(event_count), p95_events = PERCENTILE(event_count, 95) BY cluster_id | EVAL event_z_score = (event_count - avg_events) / COALESCE(stddev_events, 1), events_per_host = event_count / unique_mme_hosts | WHERE unique_mme_hosts >= 2 AND (event_z_score > 3 OR sctp_state_changes > 100) | EVAL split_brain_risk = CASE(unique_mme_hosts >= 3 AND event_z_score > 4, "CRITICAL_PARTITION_DETECTED", unique_mme_hosts >= 2 AND event_z_score > 3, "HIGH_SPLIT_BRAIN_LIKELY", "MODERATE_MONITOR") | KEEP cluster_id, datacenter, split_brain_risk, unique_mme_hosts, event_z_score, event_count, sctp_state_changes, time_bucket | SORT event_z_score DESC | LIMIT 15

relationships.note: Also joins on target_cell_id for handoff analysis
